title: The Wall Street Journal Is Lying About UFOs
summary: Jesse Michels critiques a Wall Street Journal article that claims UFO sightings at nuclear bases can be explained by secret EMP experiments and Air Force hazing rituals. He presents counter-evidence from witnesses like Robert Salas and researcher Robert Hastings, while alleging the article's sources have psychological operations backgrounds and that the WSJ refused to publish a UFO-related op-ed co-authored by a former cabinet member.

acronyms.definition: A burst of electromagnetic energy that can disrupt or damage electronic equipment
Modern term for UFOs used by government agencies
Pentagon office responsible for investigating UAP reports

events.description: General Nathan Twining stated that UFOs are not visionary or fictitious
Air Force documents discuss an emergency meeting around UFOs and nukes at Kirtland base with Army CIC, FBI, ONI, and CIA present
Don Menzel stopped astronomers at Harvard from carrying out their sky survey
Bob Jacobs UFO sighting at Vandenberg Air Force Base
Robert Salas witnessed UFO incident at Malmstrom Air Force Base where all 10 missiles went offline
Missile technician John Mills saw UFOs near Malmstrom Air Force Base
Commercial pilot in Bariloche, Argentina couldn't land plane due to outage and UFO chasing his plane

key_claims.claim: The Wall Street Journal article is part of a Pentagon disinformation campaign about UFOs
Susan Goff, a primary source for the WSJ article, has a lead role in Booz Allen Hamilton's psychological operations consulting practice for the DoD
All 10 missiles at Malmstrom Air Force Base went offline during a UFO sighting on March 24, 1967
EMPs were not operational technology until the 1970s, making the 1967 EMP explanation impossible
167 Q-cleared nuclear base employees have witnessed UFOs according to Robert Hastings' research
Boeing was contracted to investigate the Malmstrom incident and was also puzzled by what happened
A UFO-related op-ed co-authored by Karl Nell and a former presidential cabinet member was rejected by the Wall Street Journal
Sean Kirkpatrick lobbied against the UAP Disclosure Act
Don Menzel held extremely high clearances at the NSA, CIA, and Navy while publicly debunking UFOs
David Spergel serves as the Wall Street Journal's de facto adviser on UFOs
